subject: Sociology
title: Sovereignty and Globalization
description: A quick, practical guide to Sovereignty and Globalization.

hook.text: A country signs a trade deal and can no longer set its own tariffs. Did it just lose its sovereignty?

buildup.text: Sovereignty is a nation's right to govern itself. Globalization challenges this through treaties and institutions.

discovery.text: EU members share currency and regulations. WTO rules limit trade policy. Nations trade autonomy for access.

twist.text: Brexit showed sovereignty's appeal. Millions voted to leave the EU to reclaim national control — at economic cost.

climax.text: Globalization doesn't erase sovereignty — it transforms it. Nations choose which powers to share and which to keep.

quiz.question: How does globalization affect sovereignty?
options: Nations trade some autonomy for access to global systems
